messages.content: Build an Effect v3 program from scratch that opens a file-like resource inside a Scope, logs finalization order for two nested resources, and closes them in LIFO order via Effect.scoped.
Build an Effect v3 snippet from scratch showing Effect.Cache.make with a loader that fetches by key; two lookups of the same key must share one execution while different keys run independently.
Build an Effect v3 example from scratch that forks two effects into fibers, awaits both with Fiber.await, and inspects each Exit to detect success versus failure.
Build an Effect v3 program from scratch that installs a Supervisor via Effect.supervised, forks two children, and uses Supervisor.value to read the current child fiber set.
Build an Effect v3 snippet from scratch that constructs a custom Runtime from a Layer, then uses Runtime.runPromise to execute effects outside of the Effect world.
Build an Effect v3 program from scratch that acquires a mock DB connection via Effect.addFinalizer inside a scoped Effect so the release always runs — success, failure, or interruption.
Build an Effect v3 example from scratch that creates a counter metric, increments it inside a pipeline, and exposes the current value via Metric.value.
How do I control finalization order when two resources are acquired in a single Scope in Effect v3? Show a minimal example that proves LIFO order.
What's the Effect v3 way to pick between Effect.cached and Cache.make? Show a minimal example of each and a one-line comment on when each is right.
What's the Effect v3 way to fork a fiber, await it with Fiber.await, and convert the Exit into a plain success-or-error value? Show a minimal example.
What's the Effect v3 way to observe which child fibers an Effect spawns? Show a Supervisor.track minimal example.
What's the Effect v3 way to build a reusable Runtime from a Layer and run multiple effects through it? Show a minimal example.
What's the Effect v3 way to register a finalizer that runs on both success and interruption? Show a minimal example using Effect.addFinalizer and Effect.scoped.
What's the Effect v3 way to time an operation with a histogram metric? Show a minimal example using Metric.histogram and Metric.trackDuration.

Explain briefly how Effect.Scope handles finalization in Effect v3, then show a typed working implementation with two nested resources.

A Scope is a concurrent-safe collection of finalizers. Each Effect.acquireRelease registers a finalizer into the current Scope at acquire time, and when the Scope closes (Effect.scoped boundary or explicit Scope.close) it runs finalizers in reverse-acquisition (LIFO) order. This guarantees inner resources release before outer, mirroring try/finally nesting but working across async boundaries and interruption.

Explain briefly how Effect.Cache differs from Effect.cached in Effect v3, then show both with typed code.

Effect.cached memoises one parameterless Effect into an Effect<Effect<A,E,R>> — perfect for a singleton that is computed lazily and reused forever (or until the inner is rerun). Cache.make takes a lookup function keyed by input, plus capacity and TTL. It is a keyed memoisation table: per-key deduplication, per-entry expiry, bounded size. Use cached for 'load config once'; use Cache.make for 'getUserById'.

Explain briefly how Fiber.await differs from Fiber.join in Effect v3, then show a typed example using Fiber.await.

Fiber.join resumes with the fiber's value and re-raises its error or interruption into the current fiber. Fiber.await returns an Exit<E,A> without raising — you can inspect success, failure cause, or interruption explicitly. Choose await when you want to branch on failure without propagating; choose join when you want the child's error to become yours.

Explain briefly how a Supervisor observes child effects in Effect v3, then show a typed implementation using Supervisor.track.

A Supervisor is a structure that receives start/end events for every child fiber spawned under an effect and can maintain an internal value queryable via supervisor.value. Supervisor.track is a built-in that keeps the set of live child fibers. Effect.supervised(effect, supervisor) is the installer. It is non-intrusive: the effect runs the same way, but you can now ask 'how many fibers are in flight?'.

Explain briefly how a ManagedRuntime replaces ad-hoc Effect.runPromise calls in Effect v3, then show a typed example.

ManagedRuntime.make(layer) builds a runtime whose layer is shared across every run. Each subsequent runPromise/runFork call reuses the resolved services (single DB pool, single metric registry) instead of rebuilding them per invocation. When you are done, rt.dispose() releases scoped resources held by the layer. Without a managed runtime each Effect.runPromise(Effect.provide(p, layer)) pays the layer cost again and leaks scoped resources.

Explain briefly how Effect.addFinalizer differs from Effect.acquireRelease in Effect v3, then show a typed example of each.

Effect.acquireRelease pairs an acquire with its own release in a single combinator and yields the resource value. Effect.addFinalizer takes just a cleanup effect and attaches it to the current Scope without an accompanying acquire. Use acquireRelease when the resource and its release are symmetric (open/close a file). Use addFinalizer inside Effect.gen when you want to incrementally register cleanup steps (flush a log, emit a metric) as the computation progresses.
Write an Effect v3 program that opens a Scope, acquires two resources A and B, and guarantees B releases first. Constraints: strict types, no any, use Effect.acquireRelease, wrap with Effect.scoped.
Write an Effect v3 program that uses a Cache with TTL 500ms. Constraints: strict types, single loader, show that a refetch after TTL returns a fresh value by bumping a counter.
Write an Effect v3 program that forks three fibers, awaits all three with Fiber.awaitAll, and counts successes. Constraints: strict types, no any, use Exit.isSuccess.

Write an Effect v3 program that supervises a parent effect forking two children, and after the parent finishes returns the number of live fibers the supervisor still sees. Constraints: use Supervisor.track, no any.

Write an Effect v3 program that builds a ManagedRuntime from a Config layer plus a Log layer and exposes a runPromise function. Constraints: strict types, no any, two services merged with Layer.merge.
Write an Effect v3 program that uses Effect.addFinalizer to record the release Exit. Constraints: strict types, no any, wrap the whole thing in Effect.scoped.

Fix this v3 fragment so the counter metric is declared at module scope and shared across calls:
```
const handle = (u: string) => { const hits = Metric.counter("hits"); return Metric.increment(hits); };
```
Build from scratch an Effect v3 example where two Cache instances are used side by side: one for users, one for orders, with different TTLs and capacities.
Build from scratch an Effect v3 program that uses Effect.fork plus Fiber.interrupt to cancel a long-running task after a shorter timeout.
Build from scratch an Effect v3 Runtime that provides both a RandomLive layer and a ClockLive layer, then exposes a runPromise entry.
Build from scratch an Effect v3 program exposing three metrics side by side: a counter for requests, a gauge for in-flight, and a histogram for latency.
Build from scratch an Effect v3 program that uses Supervisor.fibersIn (the tracking supervisor) to count fibers that completed after a bounded parallel workload.
What's the Effect v3 way to track duration of an async step with a histogram metric? Show a minimal example.
